title: Package Morph Transition (Shrink Time-Machine)
props_required: older product package (larger / original) — filmed, or a real sourced photo
result_description: Two locked-off clips of the same product a few years apart. The older/larger package morphs (Tawel Flip lens effect) and shrinks (keyframed scale) down onto the spot the current/smaller one occupies, with a glitch + digital-error SFX on the cut and a typewriter year/weight reveal — the 'grocery store time machine'.
source: Mandu Editsx (tutorial) — adapted for FullCarts Grocery Store Time Machine

inputs.name: current_pack
inputs.what_to_film: Clip A. Tripod, locked-off. Hold the CURRENT (smaller/newer) package centered in frame and say the current year line (e.g. 'This is 2026'). Hold ~2s. Plain background, even light.

capture_requirements.framing: package centered, same distance + same spot as old_pack
capture_requirements.lighting: even, no window behind; identical to old_pack

inputs.name: old_pack
inputs.what_to_film: Clip B. SAME tripod position — do not move the camera. Hold the OLDER (larger/original) package in the EXACT same spot and say the old year line (e.g. 'And this is 2022'). Hold ~2s. If you can't get the old package, a real sourced PHOTO of it works as a held still (must be real — never AI-generated or faked packaging).

capture_requirements.framing: older package centered on the EXACT spot current_pack occupies

inputs.name: operator_endcard
inputs.what_to_film: Optional FullCarts end card: you, deadpan, delivering the CTA. Plain background so Remove Background cuts you cleanly. 3-5s.

capture_requirements.framing: head-and-shoulders, centered, room to place text beside you

params.note: Clip A (current) on track 1; Clip B (old) on track 2 directly above
cut both clips on the spoken word 'this'; delete the front half of Clip B and the back half of Clip A so playback runs A -> B
Clip B begins exactly where Clip A ends so the morph lands on one spot

params.then: nudge Position X/Y until the older package overlaps the current one perfectly, then set Opacity back to 100

edit_steps.capcut_feature: Video Effects -> Lens -> search 'Tawel Flip' -> drag onto Clip B

params.note: the morph itself; set effect duration to ~6-8 frames
at the first frame of the effect set Scale 125% + keyframe; 8 frames later keyframe Scale 100% so the big package shrinks to match

edit_steps.target: the 3-frame overlap between Clip A and Clip B
edit_steps.capcut_feature: Effects -> Glitch -> 'Bad Signal' -> drag onto the overlap

params.note: trim it to the last 3 frames of the transition

edit_steps.capcut_feature: Audio -> Sound Effects -> search 'Glitch' or 'Digital Error'

params.note: drop a glitch/digital-error SFX under the morph cut

edit_steps.target: year label that pops on the morph
edit_steps.capcut_feature: Text -> Default Text, then Animation -> In -> Typewriter

timing.cue: pop up when the package morphs

params.note: year/era label per the tutorial; swap to the real older year for the episode
FullCarts data reveal — numbers are the Kraft Mac & Cheese (Kraft Dinner) anchor; swap per episode

timing.cue: hold through the reveal + end card

params.note: match grade across Clip A + Clip B so only size/weight differs, not color/temperature

result.description: Clip A (current/smaller) cuts on the spoken word to Clip B (older/larger), which morphs via Tawel Flip and scales 125%->100% to land on the same spot, with a Bad Signal glitch + digital-error SFX on the cut and a typewriter year reveal; FullCarts adds the weight/price delta + source tag and an optional operator end card.
success_criteria: Both packages land on the exact same spot (no jump)
Size difference reads instantly; color/light match so only size changed
Glitch + SFX hit on the cut frame
Every on-screen number traces to the data anchor

source_meta.notes: Faithful to the Mandu Editsx tutorial: Tawel Flip is a Video Effects > Lens effect applied to Clip B (NOT a transition), plus a Bad Signal glitch, glitch SFX, and a Typewriter year label. FullCarts layers the weight/price reveal + source tag + optional operator end card. Per-episode: swap the year + the weight/price numbers for the documented shrink.
